title: Lambda and Functional Interfaces
learningObjectives: Explain Lambda and Functional Interfaces with advanced interview clarity
Implement Lambda and Functional Interfaces in Java with clean design
Avoid high-frequency failure patterns in Lambda and Functional Interfaces
Apply this concept to production-oriented scenarios
Communicate decisions with concise technical reasoning

sections.title: Why Lambda and Functional Interfaces matters
sections.content:
Lambdas reduce boilerplate and enable behavior-as-data style design in modern Java.

Day 29 is an inflection point between writing code that "works" and writing code that scales in maintainability and production reliability.

Interviewers at this stage evaluate:
1. Internal understanding (how it works, not only syntax)
2. Trade-off reasoning (why this approach over alternatives)
3. Runtime impact (performance, memory, contention, failure behavior)

This topic should be explained with one minimal example, one realistic failure mode, and one mitigation strategy.

If you can defend your design choices clearly, you will stand out from syntax-only answers.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Lambda and Functional Interfaces
sections.content:
### Day 29: Lambda and Functional Interfaces
Lambdas reduce boilerplate and enable behavior-as-data style design in modern Java.

### Deep understanding goal
You should understand the mechanism, its boundaries, and when to avoid it.

### Practical workflow
1. Implement baseline.
2. Add correctness checks and edge handling.
3. Evaluate runtime cost.
4. Refactor for readability and team adoption.

### Interview quality bar
- Explain what problem it solves.
- Show concise Java example.
- Name one pitfall and one mitigation.
- State complexity/runtime implication.
- Compare with one alternative.

### Production angle
In backend systems, this concept influences throughput, reliability, and debugging speed. A good implementation must support operations, not just local correctness.

### Quick self-check
- Can I explain this in <60 seconds?
- Can I code it from memory?
- Can I describe a failure scenario and fix?

### Satyverse — deeper path (theory → practice)
**Generics erase to raw types at runtime** — type parameters are compile-time checks; reflection and arrays need care (`GenericArrayCreation` errors).

**Wildcards:** `PECS` — producer extends, consumer super — guides API signatures for collections.

**Bounded type parameters** capture constraints (`T extends Comparable<T>`) — enables generic algorithms.

**Practice:** write a generic `Pair<L,R>` and a static factory — feel erasure in arrays vs lists.

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
items: Capturing mutable external state and creating hidden side effects.
Overusing lambdas where named methods improve readability.
Using checked exceptions awkwardly inside functional pipelines.
Ignoring debugging complexity in deeply chained lambdas.

sections.title: Exercise - Lambda and Functional Interfaces Applied Drill
sections.problem:
Implement a Java solution demonstrating **Lambda and Functional Interfaces** in a realistic scenario.

Requirements:
1. Include one baseline approach and one improvement.
2. Handle one edge/failure case.
3. Add short explanation of trade-off.
4. Keep code readable and testable.
5. Show sample output.
hints: Use explicit naming for intent clarity.
Write one failure-case test and expected result.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is a functional interface?
conceptual.answer: Single abstract method interface—target for lambda expressions.

wrongAnswers: Lambdas can modify any local variable they read.
Functional interface can have two abstract methods if defaults exist.
Method references always allocate fewer objects than lambdas.
You can throw checked exceptions freely from any lambda.
Anonymous inner class and lambda are identical at runtime always.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Area | Phase 4 Focus | Quick Rule | Gotcha |
|---|---|---|---|
| Concept | Lambda and Functional Interfaces | Explain mechanism + boundary | Syntax-only answers |
| Runtime | Performance/memory/concurrency | Mention impact explicitly | Hidden runtime costs |
| Code | Clean composable units | Prefer readability first | Over-chained complexity |
| Failure | Known pitfall + mitigation | Name one concrete case | Hand-wavy debugging |
| Interview | Decision reasoning | Compare alternatives | No trade-off discussion |
